name: West Point Grey
description: Community centre in West Point Grey

name: The Dugout - Hot Breakfast
hours: Daily hot meal at 10:30am. For info call (604) 685-5239.

name: Gathering Place Community Centre - Meals
hours: Meals every day of the week. Breakfast 9am-noon ($2.25), Lunch 11:30am-4:30pm ($2.75), Dinner 4:30-7pm ($3.50). Menu at https://vancouver.ca/parks-recreation-culture/gathering-place-cafeteria.aspx

name: Cityview Church - Open Table
hours: Thurs at 12pm. Hot lunch and spiritual service, more info at https://www.cityviewchurch.ca/opentable

name: DTES Neighbourhood House - Meal Program
hours: Community drop-in: Oatmeal on Mon and Wed at 10am-12pm and hot lunch on Wed at 12:30pm-2pm (open to everyone). Family drop-in: Tuesdays and Thursdays 3:30-6:30PM - afterschool snack and dinner for families/caregivers and their children. Early years drop-in: Friday 9am-11am - breakfast for families/caregivers and their children age 0-6. Peacemeal lunch: Third Sunday of every month from 12pm-1pm. Contact ops@dtesnhouse.ca or call (604) 215-2030 with any questions.

name: The Door is Open - Meal Program
hours: Anyone on Mon, Tues, Thurs, Fri, Sat at 11am and Sun at 11:30am. Wed at 9am. More info at https://www.thedoorisopen.ca/free-lunch-program/

name: Salvation Army - Harbour Light, Meal Program
Lookout Society's Powell St. Getaway - Snacks
DTES Women's Centre - Drop In - Takeout Meals
hours: Daily (except Wed) 10-11am, 12-2pm. Coffee and snacks until 4pm.

name: Quest Non-Profit Grocery Markets (Dundas)
hours: Mon–Fri 9am–6pm. Sat 10am–5pm. Closed Sunday and Holidays. Referral required to shop. For more information on referrals, please visit:  questoutreach.org/refer-clients/

name: Covenant House - Meal Program
hours: Hot breakfast M-F 9am-12pm; Hot meal or pizza M-F 3:30-5pm
description: Meals (Free). Wheelchair accessible. Serves: young people age 16-24

name: La Boussole - Foodbank
hours: Wed, 1:30pm. For information contact (604) 683-7337 or Laboussole@Lbv.Ca

name: Aboriginal Friendship Centre -Grocery Hampers
hours: An Indigenous-operated program. Delivery services are on a first come, first serve basis with limited availability.
description: Hampers (Free). Serves: Indigenous families with young children

name: Marpole Community Food Hub - Grocery Program
hours: Pickup grocery bags every Thursday, 10am-noon. Sign up at the door/contact (604) 836-7419 or foodhub@staugustinemarpole.com. Free for residents of V5W V5X V5Y V5Z V6M V6N V6P.

name: St Mary's Kerrisdale - Community Meal
hours:
Every Tuesday, 11:30AM - 12:30PM. Free, sit-down hot lunch. Hot entree or soup, salad,
bread, and sweets. Arrive 30 min prior to enjoy a coffee/tea with others. All welcome!

name: Gospel Mission Society - Food Program
hours: Serving sandwiches or hot food Wed 7-8pm

name: CityReach - Club Freedom Meal
hours: A Christ-centred worship service and hot meal. Sun 11am. For info contact jemald@cityreach.org.

name: VCH - Healthiest Babies Possible Food Support
hours: Available on the 2nd floor. Food access and other supports available. To sign up, drop in or phone Mon to Fri 8:30-4:30PM.
description: Hampers (Free). Serves: prenatal or pregnant women and youth facing challenging life circumstances

name: Tenth Church - Oasis Cafe
hours: Mon 5:30-7:30pm, Tues 11am-1pm. Dine in meals only. Access via the alley at Ontario street. For info contact oasis.cafe@tenth.ca.

name: Vancouver Community Fridge - Victoria Drive
hours: Fridge, freezer, and pantry stocked with free food and home cooked meals, located in the alley behind the green house, accessible 24/7. More info at https://vcfp.square.site/

name: Three Links Foundation - Sunday Free Breakfast
hours: Every Sunday, 9:30am to 11:30am. Warm breakfast for individuals experiencing homelessness, featuring items such as ham, egg, and cheese sandwiches, coffee, muffins, cereal, pancakes, hot chocolate, water, and juice.

name: Greater Van Food Bank - Lougheed Hwy Location
hours: Check https://foodbank.bc.ca/ for schedule (Tues, Wed, and Fri 10am-4PM, Thur 1PM - 7PM, and Sat 10AM - 3PM)
description: Hampers (Free). Wheelchair accessible. Serves: Registered clients

name: Food on the Corner
hours: Sat 11am. Soup and coffee plus a bagged lunch with PB&J sandwich, banana, granola bar. Info at https://www.foodonthecorner.ca/

name: West End Farmers Market - Nutrition Coupons
hours: Farmers Market location accepting Nutrition Coupons. Enrolled households receive minimum of $27/week in vouchers. Market is open Saturdays at 9am-2pm, from May 20th - October 28th.

name: Guru Nanak's Free Kitchen
hours: Hot meal served Saturday 1pm, Sunday 12pm outside of Carnegie Centre.

name: Seniors Lunch at Kitsilano Neighbourhood House
hours:
Tuesdays 11:00am – 1:00pm.
Light exercise, engaging conversation, entertainment and a delicious meal with dessert!
Drop-in, no registration required. 
Lunch schedule and menu can be found at https://www.kitshouse.org/seniors-55/ [kitshouse.org]
For more information contact: sarad@kitshouse.org or the front desk at Kits House. 
$7 per drop-in fee (annual $10 Kits House membership required)

name: Monthly Seniors Dine and Connect
hours: Make new friends and connect with neighbours over a free, delicious meal. Event runs monthly. The next meals will occur on July 22nd and August 19th, 6:30-8PM. Future lunches will be announced at a later date. Visit https://froghollow.bc.ca/programs-services/seniors/ for the latest information.

name: Vancouver Community Fridge - Point Grey
hours: Fridge, freezer, and pantry stocked with free food. Located inside the carpark. More info at https://vcfp.square.site/ [vcfp.square.site]. Free

name: Vancouver Community Fridge – LMNH
hours:
Fridge, freezer, and pantry stocked with free food. Temporarily located in front of the Soap Dispensary. Available 24/7. 
More info at https://vcfp.square.site/

name: The Kettle Society - Meal Program
hours: Daily @ 11:30am Must sign up in morning prior to meal service. Required to provide name and have completed member orientation to centre (Wednesdays @ 2pm). Takeout available for $0.25. Meal cost $1.

name: Japanese Community Volunteers - Meals on Wheels
hours: Japanese-style bento meal delivery service for home-bound seniors, including caregivers and people with illness or injury. Every Tuesday except two weeks during the Christmas holiday, 10:30 am – 12:30 pm. Must be home to receive the meals from delivery volunteers. An in-person pre-assessment visit from Community Services staff is required. $7/meal for Tonai Gumi members, $8/meal for non members
description: Meals (Low cost). Serves: Home-bound seniors with difficulty cooking, including caregivers and people with illness or injury

name: Bryn's Neighbourhood Table
hours: Free, sit-down dinner every Thursday 6-7pm at St. Augustine's Anglican Church. Arrive 30 min prior to enjoy a coffee/tea with others. For info contact (604) 836-7419. Wheelchair accessible. Takeout available.

name: Care BC - Western and Chinese Meals on Wheels
hours:
Western styled meals delivered weekdays 10:30 am-1pm, priced from $6.50 -9.75.
Chinese meals delivered weekdays 10:30 am-1pm, priced at $9.95.
Must be home to receive the meals. More info at https://www.carebc.ca/western-mow-vancouverrichmond.html.
description: Meals (Low cost). Serves: Home-bound older adults including frail seniors, their caregivers, adults with chronic illnesses, or with a debilitating injury

name: Evelyne Saller Centre - Low Cost Meals
hours: Breakfast 10am-12pm, Lunch 11:30am-3pm, Dinner 3-5:50pm. Menu and info at https://vancouver.ca/parks-recreation-culture/evelyne-saller-centre-cafeteria.aspx or (604) 665-3075.

name: A Loving Spoonful - Meals and groceries
hours: Eligible individuals and families can be referred by a Doctor, Nurse, Dietitian, Social Worker, or self-refer. For more information on how to refer an individual or family, please call (604) 682-6325. Referrals can be faxed to (604) 682-6327.
description: Meals (Free). Serves: individuals and families living with HIV.

name: Sheway - Lunches
hours: Mon-Fri 11:30am-1:30pm. Closed on holidays. Groceries are also available to eligible clients. For information contact (604) 216-1699 or drop in Mon-Fri between 10:30am-3pm.
description: Meals (Free). Wheelchair accessible. Serves: Sheway clients who have a current or recent history of drug and/or alcohol use and are pregnant or parenting

name: Nourishing Neighbours
hours: Free community dinner on the 2nd Monday of every month at 6PM in our Salons. Open to anyone who needs a meal.

name: Trout Lake Farmers Market - Nutrition Coupons
hours: Farmers Market location accepting Nutrition Coupons. Enrolled households receive minimum of $27/week in vouchers. Market is open Saturdays at 9am-2pm from April 1st - October 28th.

name: Quest Non-Profit Grocery Markets (Hastings)
hours: Mon-Fri: 9am-6pm. Sat 10am-5pm. Sun and holidays closed. Referral required to shop. For more information on referrals, please visit:  questoutreach.org/refer-clients/

name: Aboriginal Mother Centre - Hampers for Elders
description: Hampers (Free). Wheelchair accessible. Serves: Indigenous elders

name: DUDES Club
hours: Th at 4pm. Mainly dine-in format. Men are prioritized, and extra meals are handed out to community on a first come, first serve basis.

name: Vancouver Second Mile Society - Meal Program
Washington Community Market
hours: Low cost essential food and household supplies, Mon-Fri 9am-6pm. Sat 10am-5pm. For info contact (604) 428-9605.

name: Aboriginal Friendship Centre-Elder Meals on Wheels
hours: An Indigenous-operated program. Provides a daily meal and one hamper per month. Delivery services are on a first come, first serve basis with limited availability.

name: Covenant House - Hamper Program
description: Hampers (Free). Wheelchair accessible. Serves: youth under 25

name: First United – Community Meals
hours:
Northeast corner of Hastings St and Gore St, Vancouver
Monday – Friday, service begins around noon (not served on income assistance days or statutory holidays)
Free, hearty lunch served weekdays via the FIRST Food Truck. Usually hot meals, sometimes able to provide vegetarian option. Available to all. For info, call (604) 681-8365

name: CityReach - Food for Families (YWCA South)
hours: Nutritious foodbank. Fri, 1-2pm. Not currently accepting new registrations, more info at https://www.cityreach.org/foodforfamilies
description: Hampers (Free). Wheelchair accessible. Serves: individuals and families referred by a partner agency or school.

name: Kits House Living Room Food Distribution
hours: 11am - 12:30PM. Fresh and non-perishable food items available to take home. Randomly generated schedule to determine pick-up times. Food is available for first time drop-ins; however, regularly attending participants will be scheduled first. After your first drop-in, you can be added to the weekly randomized list and will be notified on Tuesday about your timeslot. Those without contact info can check in a few days before Friday or drop in on the day of. More info at (604) 736 3588.

name: Kitsilano Community Centre Breakfast Program
hours:
Biweekly: 2nd and 4th Saturday, 7:30am-9am. Hot breakfast until 8:45am, showers until 9am, toiletries and
clean change of clothes provided. Valet services available for carts and dogs.

name: Kits Cares - Low Cost Food Market
hours: Thurs, 12:30-2:30pm. The Market sells low-cost and subsidized produce and canned goods. Cash, debit and credit accepted. Guests can also enjoy a hot drink and a sweet treat at a cafe area at no cost. Free items of food available. For info contact kitscarescafe@tenth.ca. Operated by Tenth Church.

name: Salvation Army Boundless - Nourishing Hope Hampers
hours: Grocery hampers, pickup by appointment only.

name: CityReach - Food For Families (Slocan)
hours: Only for individuals and families referred by a partner agency or school. Nutritious foodbank. For families without minors: Tues 3-4:15pm (First and Third Tuesday of each month). For families with minors: Tues 4:30-5:30pm or Thurs 11am-noon. Not currently accepting new registrations, more info at www.cityreach.org/foodforfamilies.
description: Hampers (Free). Wheelchair accessible. Serves: individuals and families referred by a partner agency or school

name: Directions Youth Services - Meal and Snacks
hours: Open for snacks 24/7; Meal daily from 8-9 pm. Youth Pantry program Tues/Thurs/Sat, call to inquire. Intake process to become a client.
description: Meals (Free). Serves: Youth under 25 experiencing homelessness or precariously housed

name: Central Presbyterian - Rescued food distribution
hours: Located at the corner of Thurlow & Pendrell Street. Weekly distribution of rescued foods on Friday from 11:45am to 12:45PM. People are encouraged to bring their own shopping bag, clean plastic bags and a small empty container. Sliding scale entrance fee of $3-5 with 100% proceeds going to the local food bank. No one will be turned down if unable to pay.

name: Care BC - Chinese Meals on Wheels
hours: Meal delivery service for home-bound people including seniors, caregivers, people with illness or injury. Delivery times are M-F 10:30am-1pm. Must be home to receive the meals. More info at https://www.carebc.ca/chinese-mow-vancouverrichmond.html
description: Meals ($9.95). Serves: home-bound people including seniors, caregivers, people with illness or injury.

name: Jewish Family Services - JFS Kitchen
hours: Provides food support through a food bank, meal delivery program, and Jewish holiday assistance. Kosher foods available. For more info visit https://www.jfsvancouver.ca/services/family-and-adult-resources/food-and-support/ or call (604) 257-5151.

name: Kitsilano Farmers Market - Nutrition Coupons
hours: Farmers Market location accepting Nutrition Coupons. Enrolled households receive minimum of $27/week in vouchers. Market is open Sundays at 10am-2pm, from May 7th – October 29th.

name: Riley Park Farmers Market - Nutrition Coupons
hours: Farmers Market location accepting Nutrition Coupons. Enrolled households receive minimum of $27/week in vouchers. Market is open year-round, Saturdays 10am-2pm.

name: Mount Pleasant Farmers Market - Nutrition Coupons
hours: Farmers Market location accepting Nutrition Coupons. Enrolled households receive minimum of $27/week in vouchers. Market is open Sundays at 10am-2pm, May 21st – October 29th.

name: Kits Neighbourhood House Free Pantry and Fridge
hours: Take what you need and give what you can to this pantry with non-perishable foods and fridge/freezer with perishable items. Available 24/7. Located outside the neighbourhood house on Vine Street. More info at https://www.kitshouse.org/2021/11/02/kitsilano-little-free-pantry/

name: Vancouver Community Fridge - Mount Pleasant
hours: The fridge, freezer and pantry are maintained in partnership by Food Stash Foundation and the Vancouver Community Fridge Project. Food is free and everyone is welcome to take what they need and leave what they can. The Fridge is accessible 24/7 and it is in the alley between E 4th Avenue and Scotia Street at the CoFood Community Garden. More info: https://www.foodstash.ca/fridge.

name: Mount Pleasant N'hood House - Seniors Lunch
hours: Come and share a meal with your neighbours at our Seniors Lunch on Tuesdays from 12-1PM. Lunch is served at noon so it's best to come at 11:45AM. A single meal costs $5. Contact the front Desk at (604) 879-8208 for more info.
description: Meals (Low cost). Wheelchair accessible. Serves: Older adults

name: Mount Pleasant N'hood House
hours: Come and share a meal with your neighbours at our Community Lunch every second Friday from 11:45AM-1PM. Lunch is served at noon. A single meal costs $5.

name: Vancouver Community Fridge - Kerrisdale
hours: Fridge and pantry stocked with free food, located in in a brown shed to the left of the pantry area at St. Mary's. Accessible 24/7. More info at https://vcfp.square.site/ [vcfp.square.site]. Free

name: Union Gospel Mission - Meal Program
hours: Lunch every day at 11am (tickets at 10:45am). Dinner M-F at 6pm (tickets at 5:45pm), Sat-Sun at 4pm (tickets at 3:45pm). For info call (604) 253-3323.

name: Mount Pleasant N'hood House - Referral Support
hours: For information about meals and groceries available, call (604) 879-8208

name: Collingwood N'hood House - Morning Star Breakfast
hours: Sat, 7am clothing and showers, 8-8:30am breakfast. More info (604) 435-0323.

name: YWCA Crabtree Corner - Meal Programs
hours:
Breakfast: Mon-Fri, 9-10am. 
Lunch: Mon-Fri: 1-2:pm. No lunch on income assistance days.
description: Meals (Free). Wheelchair accessible. Serves: Women and children

name: St. James Community Square Pantry
hours: Take what you need and give what you can to this pantry stocked with non-perishable foods, available 24/7. Located outside the community centre on Trutch Street. More info at www.sjcommunitysquare.org/

name: Carnegie Community Centre - Cafeteria
hours: Meals served daily. Breakfast ($2.25) at 9-11am, Lunch ($2.75) at 12-4pm, Dinner ($3.50) at 5-8pm.

name: Muslim Care Centre - Meal Program
hours: Mon-Fri at 5:30pm, Sat at 1pm, Sun at Noon

name: Kiwassa Neighbourhood House - Community Lunch
hours: First & third Friday of the month, 12:15pm. 3 course meal. Seniors $7, children 12 & under $5, everyone else $8. Please inform staff during sign up if a vegetarian meal is needed. Walk-ins taken as space permits. For more info contact food@kiwassa.ca

name: Ribbon Community - Supplementary Grocery Program
hours: Groceries include protein, milk, eggs, and produce. Grocery bags available for pick up on scheduled days. See current schedule at https://www.ribboncommunity.org/grocery. To do an intake and assess eligibility, contact (604) 893-2201 and/or welcome@ribboncommunity.org
description: Hampers (Free). Serves: Community Case Management clients and participants from The Corner

name: CityReach - Food for Families (Culloden Court)
hours: Nutritious foodbank. Fri 3-4pm. Not currently accepting new registrations, more info at https://www.cityreach.org/foodforfamilies
description: Hampers (Free). Wheelchair accessible. Serves: individuals and families referred by a partner agency or school, and residents within the geographic borders of Main, Victoria, East 41st Avenue and Fraser River

name: Little Mtn N'hood House-Food Distribution Program
hours: Weekly market providing free produce and non-perishable food. Currently admitting families with kids and single parents (Limited spots).
description: Hampers (Free). Wheelchair accessible. Serves: registered individuals living within the boundaries of Cambie to Knight Street and 16th to 49th ave

name: Saige Community Food Bank - Food Hamper Pickup
hours: Two-spirit, trans and gender diverse safe space program open for all individuals in need of healthy food options. A food hamper with fresh produce and baked goods available on the 2nd and 4th Fridays of the month. www.saigecommunityfoodbank.com

name: Food Stash - Rescued Food Market
hours: Thursdays, 3-7pm fresh food market (fruit, vegetables, dairy, meat).  $24 one-time registration fee (3-month membership)

name: Vancouver Community Fridge - Kitsilano
hours: Fridge and pantry stocked with free food, located in the alley behind the red house and garden at the above address. Accessible 24/7. More info at https://vcfp.square.site/

name: Collingwood Community Fridge and Pantry
hours: Fridge and pantry stocked with free food, and located on the west facing side of Collingwood Neighbourhood House, adjacent to the gym entrance. Open 24/7. Wheelchair accessible. For more info, contact foodhub@cnh.bc.ca.

description: Free library access, internet, programs, quiet space.
